threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' without a hint of irony, and you think it's a good idea to wait for a bus in the rain.
Saw a guy on the 36 last night passionately arguing with his reflection in the window. The bus was packed, but he seemed to think he was solo.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a cat lounging atop a stack of old vinyl records outside a Späti, completely unbothered by the chaos around it.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You ever notice how in Friedrichshain, even the street art seems to have its own local gossip? Walls talking about who’s gone and who's new, like a neighborhood diary.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they instinctively dodge the BART escalator crowd like it’s Pac-Man. Tourists just stand there clueless.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Watched a guy in a gorilla suit try to board a packed Muni during rush hour. The whole bus erupted in laughter. Only in San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but they miss the authentic bars where old locals gather for vermut and jocs de taula.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still look at the metro map like it's a treasure map, puzzled at every stop instead of just riding the line.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still queue up for your coffee instead of knowing to just grab a takeaway cup from the counter. Classic newbie move.
Got on the N3 last week and the driver made an impromptu stop at a kebab shop. He said, 'Best place for midnight munchies!' We all cheered.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I found a street vendor selling fresh Falafel at 3 AM, packed with locals just laughing and chatting like it was noon.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that doubles as an art gallery. Art on the walls, döner in hand; a true Berlin experience.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know the best street taco spots are often hidden behind construction sites and random murals—tourists just stick to the main drag.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus with a guy passionately explaining his thesis on ‘90s sitcoms while the driver nodded along. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can just stroll into local bars at 10 PM for tapas. Most of us don’t even start our evening until 9:30, and half the best spots are packed by then.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their tendency to insist on learning Catalan phrases without mastering a single Spanish one first—it's a dead giveaway!
